# Fiji city guide

## Overview

Fiji is 333 islands of pure tropical bliss. Crystal-clear water, powder-white sand, coral reefs teeming with life, and the warmest, most welcoming people you'll ever meet. "Bula!" is the greeting and the attitude. Whether you want a luxury resort, a surf break, or a remote village homestay, Fiji has it.

**Best time to visit**: May-Oct (dry season) for sunny skies and calm seas. Nov-Apr is warmer, wetter, and hurricane season, but also cheaper.
**Currency**: Fijian Dollar (FJD). 2.25 FJD ≈ 1 USD.
**Language tip**: English is widely spoken. Fijian and Hindi are also official. "Bula!" (hello) is the most important word. Say it to everyone.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Nadi Town | Gateway, local | Markets, Hindu temples, airport proximity | Walk or taxi |
| Denarau Island | Resort, manicured | Luxury resorts, Port Denarau marina, golf | Taxi from Nadi (20 min) |
| Mamanuca Islands | Paradise, resort | Beach resorts, snorkeling, day trips, Castaway Island | Boat/seaplane from Denarau |
| Yasawa Islands | Remote, stunning | Backpacker to luxury, cave swimming, village stays | Yasawa Flyer from Denarau (2-5 hrs) |
| Coral Coast | Scenic, mid-range | Beach resorts, Sigatoka Sand Dunes, river tours | Drive from Nadi (1-2 hrs) |
| Suva (Capital) | Urban, cultural | Fiji Museum, markets, colonial architecture | Drive from Nadi (3 hrs) or fly |
| Taveuni | Lush, adventurous | Diving, Bouma waterfalls, International Date Line | Fly from Nadi (1 hr) |
| Pacific Harbour | Adventure | Shark diving, zip-lining, arts village | Drive from Suva (50 min) |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lovo Feast (Traditional) | Fijian | Various resorts/villages | $$-$$$ | Underground earth oven cooking. Meat and root vegetables wrapped in banana leaves, slow-cooked for hours. A communal experience. | Kokoda (Fijian ceviche), taro, lovo chicken |
| Tu's Place | Indian-Fijian | Nadi | $ | The best roti in Nadi. Fijian-Indian cuisine that reflects Fiji's multicultural heritage. Packed with locals. | Chicken curry roti, dhal, samosa |
| Nadina Authentic Fijian Restaurant | Fijian | Nadi | $$ | Traditional Fijian food in a relaxed setting. One of the few places to get authentic palusami and kokoda. | Kokoda, palusami (taro leaves in coconut), cassava cake |
| Indigo | Indian/Asian | Denarau | $$ | Upscale Indian and Asian fusion at Port Denarau. Great curries and cocktails with marina views. | Butter chicken, tandoori platter, mango lassi |
| Bonefish Seafood Restaurant | Seafood | Denarau | $$ | Fresh-catch seafood at the marina. Watch the boats while eating grilled mahi mahi. | Grilled mahi mahi, coconut prawns, Fiji Gold beer |
| Mamacita's | Mexican | Nadi | $$ | Surprisingly good Mexican food in Fiji. Fresh fish tacos and margaritas. Fun atmosphere. | Fish tacos, guacamole, margaritas |
| Nadi Market | Street food | Nadi | $ | The bustling local market with tropical fruits, curries, and Fijian snacks. Pure local experience. | Fresh mango, cassava chips, Indian sweets |
| Castaway Island Restaurant | Resort dining | Mamanuca | $$$ | Beachfront dining on a private island. Fresh seafood, tropical cocktails, sand between your toes. | Lobster, kokoda, tropical cocktail |
| Eco Cafe | Healthy/cafe | Pacific Harbour | $$ | Farm-to-table cafe in the adventure capital. Smoothie bowls, fresh juices, and local produce. | Smoothie bowl, fresh coconut water |
| Suva Municipal Market | Market | Suva | $ | Fiji's largest market. Tropical produce, kava, handicrafts, and the real Fijian food experience. | Tropical fruit, fresh kava, Fijian sweets |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mamanuca Island Hopping | Nature | Mamanucas | $$-$$$ | Full day | Boat between stunning islands. Each has its own character, from Cloud 9 floating bar to Castaway Island. |
| Shark Diving (Beqa Lagoon) | Adventure | Pacific Harbour | $$$ | Half day | Dive with bull sharks, tiger sharks, and reef sharks. Fiji is the "Shark Diving Capital of the World." |
| Cloud 9 Floating Bar | Leisure | Mamanuca | $$-$$$ | Half day | A floating platform with a bar, pizza oven, and DJ in the middle of the ocean. Surreal and unforgettable. |
| Bouma National Heritage Park | Nature | Taveuni | $ | Half day | Three stunning waterfalls in lush rainforest. The Tavoro Waterfalls trail is well-maintained and spectacular. |
| Sigatoka Sand Dunes | Nature | Coral Coast | $ | 1-2 hours | National park with massive sand dunes. Archaeological site with pottery fragments dating back 2,600 years. |
| Sawa-i-Lau Caves | Adventure | Yasawa | $$ | 2 hours | Sacred limestone caves. Swim through an underwater passage to a hidden inner chamber lit by natural light. |
| Kava Ceremony | Culture | Various | $ | 1-2 hours | Participate in a traditional kava ceremony. The drink is earthy and numbing. Clap once before, three times after. |
| Garden of the Sleeping Giant | Nature | Nadi | $ | 1-2 hours | Over 2,000 orchid varieties in a beautiful hillside garden. Peaceful and photogenic. |
| Village Visit | Culture | Various | $-$$ | 2-3 hours | Visit a Fijian village with a guide. Learn about customs, bring a gift of kava, and share a meal. |
| Snorkeling the Great Astrolabe Reef | Nature | Kadavu | $$ | Full day | One of the largest barrier reefs in the world. Pristine coral, sea turtles, and tropical fish. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cloud 9 | Floating bar | Mamanuca | $$-$$$ | The floating pizza bar in the ocean. DJ sets, swimming, and drinks in the middle of the Pacific. Day party vibes. |
| Port Denarau Bars | Bars | Denarau | $$ | The marina strip has several bars and restaurants with waterfront seating. Casual cocktails at sunset. |
| Resort Entertainment | Cultural shows | Various | Included | Most resorts host Fijian cultural nights with meke dance, fire walking, and lovo feasts. |
| Traps Bar | Bar | Nadi | $ | Popular local bar near the airport. Cheap Fiji Gold beer, pool tables, and a mix of tourists and locals. |
| O'Reilly's | Irish pub | Suva | $$ | Suva's expat bar. Cold beer, pub food, and live music on weekends. The most "nightlife" you'll find in Fiji. |

## Transit tips

- **Flights**: Fiji Airways and FijiLink connect Nadi to outer islands (Taveuni, Savusavu). Book ahead for small planes.
- **Yasawa Flyer**: Catamaran service from Denarau to the Yasawa Islands. Runs daily, book online.
- **South Sea Cruises**: Boat transfers to Mamanuca Islands from Port Denarau. Multiple daily departures.
- **Buses**: Cheap local buses run along the Coral Coast between Nadi and Suva. Slow but scenic.
- **Taxi**: Agree on a price before getting in. No meters in most places.
- **Rental car**: Useful for the main island (Viti Levu). Drive on the left.
- **Pro tip**: Build in buffer days for weather delays. Small plane and boat schedules depend on conditions.

## Useful Fijian phrases

| English | Fijian | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello! | Bula! | BOO-lah |
| Thank you | Vinaka | vee-NAH-kah |
| Thank you very much | Vinaka vakalevu | vee-NAH-kah vah-kah-LEH-voo |
| Goodbye | Moce | MOH-theh |
| Yes/No | Io/Sega | ee-OH / SENG-ah |
| Please | Yalo vinaka | YAH-loh vee-NAH-kah |
| Excuse me | Tulou | too-LOH |
| Cheers! | Bula mada! | BOO-lah MAHN-dah |
